title: trifolium sativum subsp. serotinum
intro: Trifolium sativum subsp. serotinum, also known as red clover, is a perennial legume native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its red or pink flowers and trifoliate leaves. Traditionally, the leaves and flowers of red clover have been used in herbal medicine to address various health concerns, particularly those related to the cardiovascular and digestive systems, due to their rich content of isoflavones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of red clover include potential improvements in menopausal symptoms, bone density, and cardiovascular health, as well as its antiviral and antibacterial properties. Red clover is often prepared in herbal teas, capsules, or tinctures, which can be used to support overall well-being and alleviate specific health issues.
